steps.title: Identify the proxy claim
steps.content: The hypothesis is that Ag-NP bioaccumulation in woodlice could stand in for costly sampling of vertebrates such as African clawed frogs. For that proxy to work, woodlouse measurements must predict frog measurements.

steps.title: Find what breaks the proxy
steps.content: A concerns other nanoparticles, not whether Ag-NP data can substitute for frog data. B would make woodlice more attractive for testing. D suggests stable measurements within each species. A finding that woodlouse concentrations do not predict frog concentrations directly weakens the proxy idea. The answer is C.
